Goolwa Beach · Fleurieu Peninsula · South Australia

Slow days by the Southern Ocean

Our family beach house in a prime spot at Goolwa Beach, just 200 metres from the sand. Shared with friends, family and people they vouch for. Bring the dog-eared novels and leave the laptop at home.

Check availability
The house

Room for the whole crew

A relaxed, light-filled beach house built for long weekends and lazy summer weeks. Open-plan living flows out to a deck with views over the dunes, and the beach is a 200 metre stroll from the front door.

Everything you need is here: a full kitchen, a big farmhouse table for shared dinners, a combustion heater for winter escapes, and a deck made for evening drinks while the dog watches the dunes.

Photos

Have a look around

Goolwa Beach

18 kilometres of Southern Ocean sand

Goolwa Beach is one of the great stretches of sand on the Fleurieu, running all the way to Middleton in one direction and to the mouth of the Murray in the other. It's 200 metres from the front door, and the sunsets over the water are some of the best you'll see anywhere.

Summer

Swim and surf

Patrolled by the Goolwa Surf Life Saving Club on weekends and public holidays through the warmer months. Consistent surf for all levels, with Middleton's famous breaks a short drive away.

Nov to May

Cockling

Goolwa is famous for its cockles (pipis). Wade in at low tide and do the twist. Bag and size limits apply, so grab a size guide from the local servo or fishing shop.

4WD

Drive to the Murray Mouth

Take the 4WD onto the sand and drive the beach all the way to where Australia's longest river meets the sea. Check tide times and signage at the entry point.

Year round

Walks and fishing

Long beach walks with the dog, surf casting for mulloway and salmon, and big open skies. In winter you might spot whales passing along the coast.

Around Goolwa

What's nearby

10 min drive

Goolwa township

The historic wharf precinct on the Murray, with cafes, wine bars and weekend markets. Ride the Cockle Train, Australia's oldest steel railway, along the coast to Victor Harbor, or watch the seals lazing at the Goolwa Barrage.

15 to 30 min

Coorong and Hindmarsh Island

Cross the bridge to Hindmarsh Island for the Murray Mouth lookout, or get out on the water with a Coorong cruise or kayak tour. Pelicans, dolphins and dunes as far as you can see.

15 to 40 min

Further afield

Port Elliot for Horseshoe Bay and the famous bakery, Middleton for surf lessons and winter whale watching, Victor Harbor and Granite Island for the family, and Currency Creek and Langhorne Creek cellar doors when the weather turns.

Good to know

The practical bits

Check-in and check-out
Check-in from 2pm, check-out by 10am. Flexible where we can be.
Linen
BYO linen, towels and tea towels. Doonas and pillows provided.
Cleaning
No cleaning fee, so please give the house a clean and leave it as you found it before heading off.
Pets
Well-behaved dogs welcome by arrangement. The beach is dog friendly.
Rates
Mates rates for people we know. Get in touch and we'll sort something fair.
Enquire

Keen to book a stay?

Send us a note with your preferred dates and how many are coming, and we'll get back to you with availability. If a mutual friend pointed you here, mention who.

Email Brad & Kate

We keep bookings informal and first in, best dressed. Peak periods go quickly.
